text.title: rag-eval-test-set-generation Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of rag-eval-test-set-generation enforces.

rule.statement: Balance the test set across at least 4 query types: factoid, multi-hop, summarisation, comparison. Min 25% per type.
rule.rationale: Single-type test sets miss the failures users care about.

rule.statement: At least 30% of questions come from real production logs; the rest may be LLM-synthesised.
rule.rationale: Pure synthetic sets over-fit to the generator's style.

rule.statement: Human reviews at least 20% of generated entries before the set is frozen.
rule.rationale: LLM-generated labels drift; 20% spot-check is the minimum quality gate.

rule.statement: Include at least 10% adversarial / negation / ambiguity cases.
rule.rationale: Hard cases surface failure modes that easy cases hide.

rule.statement: Tag the test set with a version + creation date + corpus snapshot hash; freeze the set for the eval campaign.
rule.rationale: Drifting test sets break cross-run comparisons.

rule.statement: Skip when an existing recent labeled set already covers query types adequately.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
